title: 10 best herbal tinctures for over bleeding during periods
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for over bleeding during periods are liquid extracts made from plants, used to alleviate excessive menstrual flow. These tinctures work by regulating hormonal imbalances and strengthening uterine contractions, reducing blood loss and discomfort. For example, the tincture of Cramp Bark (Viburnum opulus) helps to reduce cramps and spasms, while Chamomile (Matricaria chamomilla) soothes and calms the uterus. By using these herbal remedies, women can experience improved menstrual management, reduced anxiety and fatigue, and enhanced overall well-being, allowing them to live a more comfortable and empowered life during their periods.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric tinctures helps with over bleeding during periods because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce uterine contractions that can cause excessive bleeding. The active compound curcumin in turmeric also has natural platelet aggregation inhibitors, which prevent blood clots from forming and reduce the risk of heavy bleeding. Additionally, turmeric's antiseptic and astringent properties help to constrict blood vessels and promote clotting, further reducing menstrual bleeding.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Turmeric's anti-inflammatory compound curcumin helps to reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with heavy menstrual bleeding, which can contribute to reduced bleeding.
Antioxidant property: Turmeric's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals and oxidative stress that can damage the uterine lining and lead to excessive bleeding during menstruation.

Platelet-aggregating property: Turmeric contains a compound called kaempferol, which helps to increase platelet aggregation, thereby improving blood clotting and reducing the likelihood of heavy menstrual bleeding.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are a rich source of curcuminoids, which have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to reduce blood loss and alleviate menstrual cramps.
Rhyzomes: Turmeric rhyzomes contain a high concentration of curcumin, a natural platelet inhibitor that prevents excessive bleeding by inhibiting platelet aggregation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality turmeric roots, carefully selecting those that are free from mold and mildew.
Dry the harvested turmeric roots using a food dehydrator or by spreading them out in a single layer on paper towels.
Grind 100 grams of dried turmeric roots into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered turmeric with 250 milliliters of high-proof vodka, transferring the mixture to a dark glass jar with a tight-fitting lid.
Store the mixture in a cool, dark place for four weeks, shaking the jar twice daily to extract the bioactive compounds from the turmeric powder.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree tinctures helps with over bleeding during periods because of its ability to tone and regulate uterine muscles. The tannins present in the herb help to constrict blood vessels, reducing excessive flow. Additionally, Maidenhair tree has been traditionally used to stop excessive menstruation due to its astringent properties. By taking a Maidenhair tree tincture supplement during periods, women can experience relief from heavy bleeding and cramping, promoting overall menstrual health and well-being.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and Anti-Inflammatory Properties: The tannins and flavonoids in Maidenhair Tree Tincture have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce inflammation in the uterine lining and prevent excessive bleeding.
Vasoconstrictive Properties: Ginkgo biloba's active compounds, such as ginkgolic acids, can constrict blood vessels, reducing blood flow to the uterus and helping to slow down or stop heavy bleeding.

Antifibrinolytic Properties: Maidenhair Tree Tincture contains compounds that inhibit fibrinolysis, a process that breaks down blood clots. By inhibiting this process, the tincture can help reduce excessive bleeding by preventing the breakdown of blood clots in the uterus.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Maidenhair Tree contain compounds that have been traditionally used to stop excessive bleeding, making them a suitable candidate for medicinal tinctures.
Barks: The barks of the tree are rich in tannins and other compounds that can help constrict blood vessels and reduce bleeding.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and clean dry ginkgo biloba leaves according to traditional botanical practices.
Chop the ginkgo biloba leaves into small pieces and measure out 200g for this recipe.
Combine the chopped ginkgo biloba leaves with 500ml of high-proof vodka in a glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 6-8 weeks shaking the jar daily to extract the active compounds.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into another glass container and discard the solids after filtering the liquid.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chaste tree tinctures helps with over bleeding during periods because it contains a natural compound called iridoid glycosides, which help to regulate hormonal imbalances that can lead to excessive bleeding. By balancing the body's estrogen and progesterone levels, chaste tree tincture can reduce the frequency and severity of heavy menstrual flows. Additionally, its anti-inflammatory properties may also help to ease discomfort and cramping associated with heavy periods, providing a natural and holistic approach to managing menstrual symptoms.
remedy_properties: Estrogen-regulating properties: Chaste tree tincture helps regulate estrogen levels, which can help reduce heavy bleeding by balancing hormone fluctuations.

Anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects: The tincture's anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties can help soothe the uterine lining and reduce inflammation, alleviating cramps and heavy bleeding associated with menstruation.
Oxytocin-modulating properties: Chaste tree tincture may also modulate oxytocin levels, a hormone involved in uterine contractions, which can help reduce excessive menstrual flow.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in iridoid glycosides, which have astringent and anti-inflammatory properties that help to stop excessive menstrual bleeding.
Barks: The barks contain flavonoids and terpenes, which have antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory effects, reducing uterine cramps and heavy bleeding.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried vitex agnus castus leaves and flowers in quantities of about one pound.
Grind the dried herb into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or coffee grinder.
Combine 10 grams of the powdered vitex with 100 milliliters of 95% ethanol in a clean glass container.
Allow the mixture to macerate for 14 days, shaking the container daily, before straining it through cheesecloth.
Transfer the filtered tincture to dark glass bottles and store it in a cool place for at least 2 weeks before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Guelder rose tinctures helps with over bleeding during periods because of its rich composition of antioxidants, flavonoids, and phenolic acids. These bioactive compounds have been traditionally used to regulate menstrual flow and alleviate symptoms associated with heavy bleeding. The astringent properties of guelder rose help to contract blood vessels, reducing excessive bleeding, while its anti-inflammatory effects soothe the uterine lining, promoting a healthy balance of hormones. As a result, guelder rose tinctures can provide relief from the distress and discomfort caused by over bleeding during periods.
remedy_properties: Vasoprotective and anti-inflammatory effects: The flavonoids present in Guelder Rose tincture help to protect blood vessels from damage, reducing inflammation and congestion that can lead to heavy bleeding during menstruation.

Antioxidant activity: Guelder Rose's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals that can contribute to oxidative stress and tissue damage, which may exacerbate heavy bleeding during periods.

Hormone-regulating effects: Guelder Rose has been traditionally used to support the female reproductive system and regulate hormonal imbalances, which may help to reduce heavy menstrual bleeding by normalizing uterine contractions and blood flow.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used due to their ability to regulate menstrual flow and reduce excessive bleeding, making them an effective natural treatment for heavy menstruation.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes, also known as underground stems, help to stop bleeding by reducing inflammation and improving circulation, which can alleviate heavy menstrual bleeding.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried viburnum opulus flowers and leaves from a trusted source in sufficient quantities for the desired tincture batch.
Clean and chop the herbs into small pieces to ensure proper extraction of their medicinal properties.
Combine one part dried herb material with three parts high-proof vodka or 80% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for four to six weeks, shaking daily, to allow for optimal extraction and maturation.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids and labeling the tincture with ingredients dosages preparation time and recommended use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Black cohosh tinctures helps with over bleeding during periods because it contains a unique combination of phytoestrogens that can help regulate hormonal imbalances, particularly estrogen dominance. This can lead to a reduction in heavy menstrual flow and clotting, providing relief from discomfort and anxiety associated with excessive bleeding. By stabilizing hormone levels, black cohosh tinctures may also alleviate cramping and mood swings, promoting a more balanced and comfortable menstrual cycle.
remedy_properties: Estrogenic compounds: Black cohosh contains estrogenic compounds that can help to regulate menstrual bleeding and reduce excessive uterine contractions, leading to lighter and more manageable periods.

Vasopressin-like effects: Black cohosh has been shown to have vasopressin-like effects, which means it can help to constrict blood vessels and reduce blood flow, thereby reducing heavy or prolonged menstrual bleeding.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in black cohosh may help to reduce uterine inflammation and swelling, which can contribute to excessive bleeding during menstruation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the black cohosh plant contain a high concentration of triterpenoid saponins, which are responsible for its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, making it effective in reducing menstrual bleeding.

Rhyzomes: Black cohosh rhyzomes have been traditionally used to treat gynecological issues, including heavy bleeding during menstruation, due to their ability to tone the uterine muscles and reduce inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried actaea racemosa roots in the summer months for optimal potency.
Chop the roots into small pieces and combine with vodka at a ratio of one part herb to two parts solvent.
Steep the mixture in a glass jar with a lid for 6-8 weeks, shaking daily, to facilitate extraction.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding solids.
Store the tincture in a cool dark place and use within one year of preparation, dosing as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dong quai tinctures helps with over bleeding during periods because it contains a unique blend of herbal compounds that help to regulate and balance the body's hormone production. The tincture's primary active ingredient, Angelica sinensis, is known for its ability to strengthen and tone the uterine lining, reducing excessive blood flow and promoting normal menstrual cycles. Additionally, Dong quai's anti-inflammatory properties help to alleviate cramping and discomfort associated with heavy bleeding, providing relief and a sense of calm during this time.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Dong Quai's antioxidant properties help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the uterus, which can contribute to heavy menstrual bleeding.
Vasodilatory effects: The saponins present in Dong Quai have been shown to dilate blood vessels, reducing blood flow to the uterus and thereby alleviating heavy menstrual bleeding.

Stabilization of uterine contractions: Dong Quai's flavonoids help regulate uterine contractions, which can reduce excessive bleeding by stabilizing the contractions and preventing prolonged bleeding.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in saponins, which help to stabilize blood vessels and reduce bleeding.
Barks: The barks contain astringent compounds that aid in contracting blood vessels and stopping excess bleeding.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality angelica sinensis roots and leaves at dawn on a sunny day.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm, dry place for seven to ten days.
Combine one part dried angelica sinensis with three parts vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for six to eight weeks, shaking the jar twice daily for optimal extraction.
Strain and filter the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into dark glass bottles.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Red clover tinctures helps with over bleeding during periods because of its rich content of isoflavones, specifically biochanin A and formononetin. These compounds have been shown to help regulate hormonal balance, particularly estrogen levels, which can contribute to heavy menstrual flow. The tincture's ability to stabilize blood clotting factors and reduce inflammation also helps to alleviate bleeding issues. Additionally, red clover has a natural anti-inflammatory effect on the uterine lining, further reducing menstrual cramps and excess bleeding. As a result, many women find that herbal red clover tinctures provide effective relief from excessive menstrual bleeding.
remedy_properties: Estrogenic effects: Red clover contains isoflavones, which can help to balance and regulate estrogen levels in the body, reducing heavy menstrual bleeding by promoting uterine health and slowing down blood flow.

Vasoprotective properties: The saponins present in red clover tincture have been shown to strengthen and stabilize blood vessels, which can help to reduce bleeding by preventing capillaries from breaking and leaking excessively.

Anti-inflammatory effects: Red clover's flavonoids and phenolic acids exhibit anti-inflammatory activity, which can help to reduce prostaglandins (hormones that cause uterine contractions and bleeding) and alleviate symptoms of heavy menstrual bleeding.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the red clover plant contain a high concentration of bioactive compounds, including isoflavones and phenolic acids, which help to regulate menstrual bleeding and reduce excessive flow.
Buds: The buds of the red clover plant are rich in flavonoids and saponins, which have been traditionally used to treat menstrual disorders, including heavy bleeding and cramps.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh red clover flowers in full bloom at dawn or early morning.
Weigh out 100 grams of the harvested flowers and chop them finely to release their essential oils.
Combine the chopped flowers with 500 milliliters of pure grain alcohol in a clean glass jar and stir well.
Allow the mixture to macerate for four weeks, shaking the jar daily, before straining and filtering the tincture.
Transfer the filtered tincture to dark glass bottles and store it at room temperature for up to two years after preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint tinctures helps with over bleeding during periods because it has a natural ability to reduce inflammation and slow down blood flow. The menthol in peppermint acts as a vasoconstrictor, constricting blood vessels and thereby reducing the amount of blood lost during menstruation. Additionally, peppermint's anti-inflammatory properties help to soothe the uterine lining, further alleviating heavy bleeding. By taking herbal peppermint tinctures, women can experience a significant reduction in menstrual flow, making them feel more comfortable and confident throughout their period.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Herbal peppermint tincture helps to relax the uterine muscles, reducing cramping and spasms that can contribute to heavy menstrual bleeding, thereby easing the flow of blood.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Peppermint's anti-inflammatory compounds help to reduce inflammation in the uterus, which can cause excessive bleeding by thinning the lining of the womb, thereby promoting a more balanced menstrual cycle.

Antioxidant and vasodilatory effects: The antioxidants in peppermint tincture help protect the body from oxidative stress caused by heavy menstruation, while its vasodilatory properties widen blood vessels, reducing blood pressure and thus easing bleeding.
remedy_parts: Roots: Peppermint roots have been traditionally used to stop excessive menstrual bleeding due to their ability to constrict blood vessels and reduce inflammation.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of the peppermint plant contain a high concentration of menthol, which helps to ease cramps and reduce heavy bleeding by relaxing uterine muscles.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves and rinse them gently under cold running water.
Combine 200g of peppermint leaves with 500ml of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to steep for 2 weeks in a cool dark place shaking the jar daily.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another clean glass bottle discarding the solids.
Store the tincture in the refrigerator at a temperature below 40°f and use within 6 months after opening.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Evening primrose tinctures helps with over bleeding during periods because of its unique ability to support uterine health. The rich source of gamma-linolenic acid (GLA) in herbal evening primrose oil helps to reduce inflammation and promote healthy blood flow, thus alleviating excessive menstrual bleeding. Additionally, GLA has been shown to regulate prostaglandins, which play a crucial role in menstruation. By balancing these hormones, the tincture can help to normalize uterine contractions and minimize heavy bleeding associated with PMS.
remedy_properties: Gamma-Linolenic Acid (GLA): GLA, an omega-6 fatty acid, helps to regulate prostaglandins in the body, which can reduce inflammation and tone down uterine contractions, thereby reducing excessive bleeding.

Vitex Agnus-Castus (Chaste Tree): Vitex has a regulatory effect on the hormonal balance in the body, particularly the prostaglandin levels, which can help to normalize uterine bleeding patterns and reduce heavy menstrual flow.

Cytokinin: Cytokinin, a plant hormone found in evening primrose, helps to regulate cell division and growth, which can aid in reducing inflammation and tissue damage that may contribute to excessive bleeding.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in estrogenic compounds, making them effective in regulating menstrual bleeding and reducing heavy flow.
Seeds: Containing gamma-linolenic acid (GLA), a fatty acid with anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce bleeding and ease cramps.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of fresh oenothera biennis flowers on a dry sunny day for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested flowers in a warm oven or dehydrator at 180°f for 30 minutes.
Combine 1 part dried oenothera biennis with 5 parts high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar, filling to the top.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking daily, allowing the herbal goodness to infuse into the liquid.
Strain and filter the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a dark glass bottle, discarding the solids and labeling for use as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bell pepper tinctures helps with over bleeding during periods because of its rich source of vitamin C, antioxidants, and anti-inflammatory compounds. These natural properties help to regulate menstrual flow, reduce inflammation in the uterine lining, and improve blood clotting. Additionally, bell peppers contain flavonoids that have been shown to strengthen capillaries and prevent excessive bleeding. As a result, herbal bell pepper tinctures can provide relief from heavy bleeding, cramps, and discomfort associated with over bleeding during periods.
remedy_properties: Vitamin K-rich herbs like Turmeric: Turmeric contains vitamin K, which plays a crucial role in blood clotting and can help reduce excessive bleeding.
Anti-inflammatory herbs like Chamomile: Chamomile has anti-inflammatory properties that may help soothe the uterine lining and reduce inflammation, which can contribute to heavy menstrual bleeding.

Uterotonic herbs like Raspberry Leaf: Raspberry leaf is known for its uterotonic properties, which can help tone the uterus and reduce excessive bleeding by increasing blood clotting and strengthening uterine contractions.
remedy_parts: Roots: Bell pepper roots are valued for their astringent properties, which can help to stop excessive bleeding and reduce menstrual flow.
Barks: The bark of the bell pepper plant is used to create a tincture that helps to constrict blood vessels and reduce bleeding.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality capsicum annuum leaves at peak ripeness for optimal potency.
Chop the harvested leaves into fine pieces and measure out 100 grams by weight.
Combine the chopped capsicum annuum leaves with 500 milliliters of 40% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 14 days, shaking daily to ensure even extraction.
Strain and filter the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass container, discarding solids.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat over bleeding during periods with herbal tinctures is to combine a few drops of Chamomile tincture with a pinch of dried Sage leaves. Take this mixture three times daily as needed. The anti-inflammatory properties of Chamomile will help reduce inflammation and ease discomfort, while the astringent properties of Sage will constrict blood vessels and slow down bleeding.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to over bleeding during periods that are treated with herbal tinctures are various uterine and gynecological issues. These include menorrhagia, dysmenorrhea, endometriosis, fibroids, and polycystic ovary syndrome (PCOS). Herbal tinctures like Angelica, Ashwagandha, Chamomile, Dong Quai, and Ginger have been used to ease symptoms such as heavy bleeding, cramping, and hormonal imbalances. They also help regulate the menstrual cycle and alleviate PMS symptoms.
